programme.desc: After a plane crash paralyzes Jack and kills his dad, his stepmom abandons him. A kind doctor becomes his foster mom, helping him walk again. Jack then chooses to become a doctor, ultimately forgiving and helping his estranged stepmom.

programme.title: Stepbrothers Love Same Girl
programme.desc: Abused by his stepfamily, brilliant Ren hides his potential and suffers bullying. His teacher, Elias, uncovers his genius and past, helping him escape his toxic home and embrace his true potential, leading to a scholarship at Harvard.

programme.title: Millionaire Calls Out Girl With Fake Purse
programme.desc: Francesca, an aspiring broker, fakes her credentials to impress clients and support her ailing mother. When her deception is exposed by a rival, a sympathetic client offers her a life-changing opportunity, allowing Francesca to finally fulfill her promise to her mom.

programme.title: Girl Takes Best Friend's Boyfriend
programme.desc: At an elite school, scholarship student Grace challenges the "R3" rich bullies. After they retaliate, she uncovers a softer side to their leader, Benny, and inspires him to change, leading to a surprising romance.

programme.title: Stepmom Takes Inheritance
programme.desc: After a plane crash paralyzes Jack and kills his dad, his stepmom abandons him. A kind doctor becomes his foster mom, helping him walk again. Jack then chooses to become a doctor, ultimately forgiving and helping his estranged stepmom.

programme.title: Millionaire Teen Turns Broke Overnight
programme.desc: Spoiled millionaire Ethan, grieving his mom, is forced to spend a night in her childhood home—a low-income apartment. This humbling experience, coupled with Mrs. Davis's kindness, transforms his perspective, inspiring him to use his wealth to help others.

programme.title: Rich Step Dad Won't Share Money
programme.desc: Natalie, tired of her superficial life and stepdad's control, convinces her mom Martha to leave her cheating husband. They face financial struggles but find strength and independence together, starting a new, authentic life.

programme.title: Poor Sisters Not Allowed To Eat
programme.desc: The sudden death of their mother leaves Laura and Ana and their father, Miguel, struggling with grief and poverty. For Thanksgiving, the girls secretly salvage their dad's ruined dinner, reminding him that family makes it work, catching the attention of a wealthy party host.

programme.title: My Teenage Wedding

programme.title: My Teenage Wedding

programme.title: Making a Model with Yolanda Hadid

programme.title: Making a Model with Yolanda Hadid

programme.title: Tom Kerridge

programme.title: Tom Kerridge

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Chris Fuller, a former British Army Bomb Disposal Officer, who now does humanitarian work in the Middle East, talks to us about defusing bombs in the centre of London, and the difference between working in the UK and abroad.

programme.title: Honesty Box
programme.desc: Deena Owen, Paramedic and Hospital Ambulance Liaison Officer, answers all your questions, revealing the worst bodily fluid to deal with, and why there are different sirens, and recalling the shocking moment she was stabbed twice by a patient!

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: In this weeks episode of Minutes With we sat down with Mirsad Solakovic, a Bosnian refugee who was forced to flee to the UK with his family. Mirsad tells us about the torture he suffered at the hands of his Serbian school teacher, the time he spent in a concentration camp and how this gave him PTSD.

programme.title: Snack Wars
programme.desc: In this episode of Snack Wars, mgk puts classic British and American snacks to the test! After a couple of sausage-based innuendos, mgk feels like a proper British gentleman with a cup of Earl Grey tea and teaches us all about the 'Pretzel plant'. Former England Lionesses Mille Bright and Rachel Daly are the latest footballers to play a game of Snack Wars: England vs The Rest of Europe. Ahead of the UEFA Women's Euros 2025, Bright and Daly try marmite and compare pickled walnuts to sauerkraut.

programme.title: Snack Wars
programme.desc: Zach Galifianakis and Billy Magnussen Rate UK & USA Snacks | Liam Neeson and Pamela Anderson Try Northern Irish & Canadian Food

programme.title: Snack Wars
programme.desc: Get ready for the ultimate snack showdown as Hollywood stars Margot Robbie and Colin Farrell go head-to-head taste-testing their nations' most iconic treats, and things get competitive... Fast.

programme.title: Do You Even Know Me?
programme.desc: Margot Robbie and Rami Malek put their friendship to the test in this brilliant episode of Do You Even Know Me? We learn about Margot's love for Harry Potter, Rami's unusual job and the amazing prank Margot pulled as a child!

programme.title: Snack Wars
programme.desc: Anya Taylor-Joy and Alexander Skarsgård choose between UK and Swedish vegan snacks in this entertaining episode of Snack Wars! They compare Greggs sausage rolls to Swedish meatballs and fruit gums to a Swedish snack called Djungelvrål. Zac Efron and Ryan Kiera Armstrong compare UK and USA snacks! Zac has a hilarious reaction to Toxic Waste, Ryan tries her first-ever Twinkie, and they both can't get enough of British snacks!

programme.title: Do You Even Know Me?
programme.desc: See how well A Complete Unknown stars Timothée Chalamet and Edward Norton know each other! Timothée speaks about his love of the Hull accent and quotes iconic Geordie Shore memes, while Edward discusses being related to Pocahontas.

programme.title: Swipe Stuff
programme.desc: Louis Tomlinson swipes yes or no on everything from Disney adults and Aperol Spritz to avocado toast, Abba and Labubus in this hilarious episode of Swipe Stuff! He gives his honest opinion on everything with no room for middle ground!

programme.title: Agree to Disagree
programme.desc: In this hilarious episode, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson and Kevin Hart do amazing impressions of each other, Dwayne reveals a naughty secret about Kevin and we find out their thoughts on The Rock becoming US president!

programme.title: Would You Rather?
programme.desc: Spurs supporters go head-to-head in a fiery Would You Rather debate, tackling tough scenario-based questions such as: Spurs win the Premier League but Arsenal win the Champions League or both teams win nothing? What will they decide?!

programme.title: The Eleven
programme.desc: In this episode of The Eleven, Manchester United fan Adam McKola goes up against Liverpool fan Grizz Khan to debate an all-time Premier League era combined team, with Thogden overseeing proceedings.

programme.title: Don't Take the Bait
programme.desc: Ahead of Liverpool v Man United, Liverpool fan Laurence McKenna and Manchester United fan Harry Pinero try and get each other to nibble over some of the biggest debates between the two Premier League rivals! Loser faces a fishy forfeit!

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: In the second part of our interview with Jamie Morgan Kane, he speaks about his time in some of the most violent prisons in the USA, his interactions with dangerous serial killers Charles Manson and Ed Kemper, and his eventual journey to freedom.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Patrick Foster's gambling addiction had catastrophic consequences on his career and family life. Here, he tells us about his internal battle with addiction, the millions he's placed in bets, and the vast sums of debt that he racked up as a result.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Professor Patricia Wiltshire's research into pollen and plants has helped convict some of the UK's most notorious killers. Here, she tells us about the cases that have stayed with her during her 30-year career, and the one that brought her to tears.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Former New York Mafia boss Michael Franzese was a caporegime in the Colombo crime family. In this episode, he tells us how he made a fortune in his scheme to defraud the federal government out of gasoline taxes and how he turned his life around.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: In this episode of Minutes With we sat down with Gary, whose cocaine addiction led to him spending £200 a day on drugs whilst working as a police officer. After losing his job in the force, Gary turned to crime to feed his addiction and stole thousands from betmakers and supermarkets. Gary tells us about his battle with addiction, his road to recovery and how he's turned his life around.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: John Massey, at one point England's longest-serving prisoner, spent an astonishing 43 years behind bars. John candidly shares his life story with us, including the murder that led him to prison, his four daring escapes, and his eventual release.

programme.title: Bewitched S3E87
programme.desc: Finding Samantha struggling to repair a lamp, Aunt Clara uses her uncertain powers of magic to summon an electrician. Benjamin Franklin, in l8th century clothes, appears. Samantha is upset by Ben's presence and Aunt Clara's wail that she can't remember how to undo the spell. Darrin, astonished by Ben's appearance in his home, can't convince Larry Tate that Ben is not is creation for an advertising campaign for Franklin Elec-tronics. Abner Kravitz refuses to believe Gladys' report that the real Ben Franklin is at the Stephens'. Bowing to Samantha's pleas, Darrin agrees to allow her and Aunt Clara to show Ben how his inventions are being used is the city. The next morning Ben, leaving a note that he has decided to tour the city alone to avoid causing domestic discord, sets out. Darrin, Samantha and Aunt Clara start searching for him. At a firehouse, inspecting an old fire engine, Ben steps on the accelerator. The engine shoots out into the street and slams into a curb. Ben is arrested and taken to court, where the judge, sure it is all a publicity stunt, charges him with grand larceny and sets bail at a thousand dollars. Darrin puts up the bail, knowing he will lose it if Ben doesn't appear for trial is a month. Just then, Aunt Clara remembers the spell and Ben vanishes.

programme.title: The Biblical Plagues
programme.desc: The Biblical Plagues - Darkness over Egypt: For a long time, scientists have tried to prove that nine of the Ten Plagues described in the Old Testament point to a serious breakdown of the climate, triggering an ecological chain reaction. Apart from theological considerations, scholars all over the world have studied individual phenomena, trying to explain the causes of this Biblical disaster.

programme.title: The Biblical Plagues

programme.title: Monsters and Myths

programme.title: Return of the Bible Plagues
programme.desc: Return of the Bible Plagues - Deadly Waters: “... all the waters that were in the river [Nile] were turned to blood. And all the fish that was in the river died,” reports the Bible, “and the river stank, and the Egyptians could not drink of the water of the river.” (Exodus 7:20–21).

programme.title: Return of the Bible Plagues
programme.desc: Return of the Bible Plagues - Mosquitos: “… so there were lice upon man, and upon beast,” reports the Bible, “all the dust of the land became lice throughout all the land of Egypt.” (Exodus 8:18, 17). Even today, two million people die every year from malaria transmitted by mosquitoes, mainly in Africa.

programme.title: Return of the Bible Plagues
programme.desc: Return of the Bible Plagues - Hailstorm: “… and the Lord sent thunder and hail, … and fire mingled with the hail,” reports the Old Testament. “And the hail smote … all that was in the field, both man and beast.” (Exodus 9:23–25).

programme.desc: Millions of people believe extraterrestrial live on Earth. Are the aliens here to enlighten us or destroy us? What if the worst-case scenario happened? What would aliens do? And how would they do it?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: They're our first line of defense in the darkest of times. And they're often the first responders to threats from another world. Over the past 70 years the world's military forces have come face to face with thousands of UFOs.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Every day, millions take to the sky in commercial flights all over the world. But decades of evidence have revealed we may not be the world's only frequent flyers. Are we in danger of losing control of our skies to extraterrestrial beings?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: It's the stuff of nightmares. Emergency lines flooded with calls as UFOs appear in the sky. Mounting evidence has revealed that UFO waves are able to penetrate the world's most closely guarded airspace.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: It's the ultimate close encounter. There have been thousands of reported cases all over the world.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: UFOs. They can fly at blinding speed or hover effortlessly just a few feet above the ground. And they can do it all without making a sound. How is this possible?

programme.desc: Take an exotic location, add a mystery and someone determined to solve it and you get Legend Hunters: a show that follows a real-life Indiana Jones to the ends of the earth and back searching for clues to prove that legends are real.

programme.title: S1 E3 The End Of The Earth: Deep Space Threats To Our Planet
programme.desc: NASA's top brass and other scientists are arming themselves.

programme.title: S2 E6 Dark Matter
programme.desc: Dark Matter and Dark Energy make up 96% of the Universe.

programme.title: S2 E8 Space Travel
programme.desc: Explore the science behind the seemingly fanciful notion of warp-drive.

programme.title: S3 E12 Cosmic Phenomena
programme.desc: A variety of cosmic events have both helpful and harmful effects on life on Earth.

programme.title: S5 E4 Time Travel
programme.desc: One of the Universe's most enduring mysteries is Time Travel.

programme.title: S5 E6 Asteroid Attack
programme.desc: What are the latest discoveries in the deadly world of asteroids?

programme.title: S5 E7 Total Eclipse
programme.desc: Total Solar Eclipses are one of the most spectacular phenomena in the heavens.

programme.title: S1 E1 San Andreas Fault
programme.desc: The San Andreas Fault runs roughly 800 miles.

programme.title: Close to Death
programme.desc: One of the oddest natural adaptations is super-deep sleep. These near-death experiences feature animals temporarily stopping their hearts and brains others can hibernate for months at a time while some can freeze themselves, employing a natural anti-freeze to keep their cells intact. But as we explore this natural time travel, we also discover that this lifestyle is not without risks…

programme.title: Glorious Grasslands
programme.desc: Savanna, prairie, meadow: grassland goes by many names. Seas of green, dominated by one of the most successful plant species. It's so hardy and productive that it can survive flooding, drought and fires, managing to feed vast animal communities. What's more, it's not just a food source, it's also a theatre many of nature's most dramatic spectacles play out in this breathtaking wilderness. We literally need grasslands to breathe, but they're a vital resource for some of our most exciting wildlife!

programme.title: Lion
programme.desc: The endless plains of East Africa is a paradise for lions. The most social of all cats, lions form prides that are unlike any other animals. Together, they'll hunt, raise their young and defend their territory. With such a close family and plentiful home, the pride welcomes new babies every year. But new children introduces new dangers. The cubs will have to trust their instincts, their mother and the power of the pride if they are to make it to adulthood and take their place on the African plains.

programme.title: Bucketlist Africa
programme.desc: Africa: one of the most iconic and diverse continents. It's packed with a spectacular range of habitats and environments, and home to some of the best loved and dramatic wildlife on the planet. From the small and surprising to the enormous and entertaining, there's something for everyone's bucket list on this vast continent. From camels to giraffes, gorillas to buffalo, the sheer range of wildlife found in Africa is unbelievable. With so much to see, where would you begin your bucket list adventure?

programme.title: Monkey Mayhem
programme.desc: Monkeys: loveable, aggressive, diverse and full of mischief. Our distant cousins have much in common with us. They're intelligent, cooperative and have complex relationships. They're some of the most evolutionary successful species on the planet, successful across the globe from Japanese snow monkeys to African baboons. Swinging through our family tree quickly reveals the diversity of our monkey relatives. They come in all shapes and sizes, and are certainly not as different from us as we might think.

programme.title: World's Rarest
programme.desc: The world is facing an urgent biodiversity crisis. Untold numbers of animals are at risk of being lost forever, with many species racing towards extinction. In this show, we try to highlight some of these incredible creatures, from the plucky pangolins to the critically endangered rhinoceroses, who live in constant fear of the poacher's bullet. Films provide a record of Earth's wonderful wildlife, but this is hopefully not an archive of what's lost, but evidence of what we should be preserving.

programme.title: Rivers Lifeblood
programme.desc: Rivers flow like veins across the Earth, a lifeline to countless ecosystems. Since the dawn of time they've fuelled the development of animal and human life alike. Habitats in constant flux, they change with the seasons, gradually transforming over time. As we run the river's course, we meet the spectacular animals who depend on it. These waterways are as much as lifeblood to this wildlife as they are to us. They deserve our respect and protection – they're our lifeblood and we must keep them flowing.

programme.title: mareTV
programme.desc: The island of Martinique is a piece of France in the Caribbean, between Florida and South America. And of course, Savoir-vivre applies just as it does on the other side of the Atlantic, between the English Channel and the Mediterranean. Jacky Pascault is the first and only man to cultivate mushrooms in the Caribbean. Everywhere else, they grow on artificially fertilised soil, but Jacky's Champignons Tropicaux flourish on sugar cane fibres. Nicole Vilo tailors hats and dresses out of coconut palms. Of course, the catwalk for the models wearing her first collection is one of the countless dream beaches. The Martiniquais sail on boats they call Yole, which have no keel and are therefore difficult to navigate. The crew members hang over the water in breakneck fashion on long wooden poles. Just the job for Yole-boss Max' "problem kids": they not only learn complicated handling, but also team spirit.

programme.title: mareTV
programme.sub-title: Curacao, Blue Wonder of the Caribbean
programme.desc: Curacao is one of the most popular attractions for countless cruise ships on their trips through the Caribbean, because it is located outside of the hurricane belt. This is why Eric Miguel is constantly in action. He is the bridge keeper of the Queen Emma Bridge, the landmark of Curacao's capital, Willemstad. After his shift, Eric goes to Yvonne Troeman's cook-shop. Her speciality is Kadushi, a cactus soup. The ingredients include cactus flesh, pigtail's, mussels and a few secret "treats". Barbara van Bebber is the only submarine pilot on Curacao and literally gets to the bottom of things amidst the turquoise-blue wonder of the Caribbean. She charters out her sub to marine biologists and wealthy private explorers, simultaneously cleaning up on the seabed. Her "Curasub" has claw arms, which she employs to remove anything that doesn't belong down there: beer bottles, car tyres, and plastic refuse. Jeroen Eikelenboom simply carts sand from a neighbouring cove to his own surf club in his favourite bay.

programme.title: Caribbean sisters: The Lesser Antilles St. Kitts & Nevis
programme.desc: In the centre of the Caribbean, volcanoes rise steeply from the sea, thus shaping the sister islands of St. Kitts & Nevis. Their slopes, covered in lush rainforests, snow-white, sandy beaches and the surrounding tur-quoise tinted waters provide the ultimate setting for this record-breaking, albeit tiny island paradise: Both islets are the smallest of the Caribbean dwarf states and also rank among the world's ten smallest states. A hot pepper sauce, made of a creative mixture of ginger, garlic, thyme and extraordinarily hot chillies that thrive in the hot and humid climate of Nevis, is simmering on the stove of Violet and Llewellyn's house. Lemmy Pemberton digs up abandoned turtle nests with his bare hands, as he wants to be certain that all of the babies have indeed managed to make it. Members of the Turtle Group catch sea turtles in order to take their measurements and fit them with transmitters. Taxi bus-es are not only the main means of transport on St. Kitts & Nevis, they are also an artistic expression of each driver's personality. Colourfully embellished, each taxi represents island art on wheels.

programme.title: glCuba
programme.desc: Even anyone who has not yet been to Cuba can conjure up certain images of American vintage street cruisers, imposing buildings that have seen better days, white sandy beaches full of palms and loving couples on the Malecón against a backdrop of crashing waves. This island is somewhat of a myth and its people generate a special joie de vivre. The legendary Hershey Train departs for Matanzas from the capital, Havana. On the oldest electrified railway line in the Caribbean, 60-year-old Spanish carriages make their way along al-most 100 kilometres of coastline in the most relaxed way possible. The Salvavidas on Varadero, probably the most famous beach on the island, have a particular-ly beautiful workplace. The lifeguards' territory ex-tends for more than 20 kilometres. In order to protect the coasts of Cuba, mangroves have been replanted here for many years. The project is supported by the UN and planting is often carried out by schoolchildren. In doing so, Cuban children learn how to treat their maritime habitat in a sustainable fashion. The former colonial city of Trinidad on Cuba's south coast is gen-erally considered to be one of the most beautiful places in the entire Caribbean. The houses here are particular-ly colourful. A plethora of magnificent buildings can be seen at the Plaza Mayor, the neo-baroque main square.

programme.title: mareTV
programme.sub-title: The Caribbean for explorers: St. Vincent and the Grenadines
programme.desc: Much more Caribbean than on the islands of St. Vincent and the Grenadines is hardly possible. Deserted beaches, uninhabited islands, lush vegetation, the 33 islands in total are dreamlike with extremely hospitable inhabitants. Miranda Phillips runs a laundry and water boat on Bequia. Every morning, the 32-year-old navigates from yacht to yacht with her small bright green service vehicle and is already expected by most skippers. Miranda promises radiantly fresh laundry, which she delivers again the same day. And so there is always something going on in her tiny laundrette in the harbour. It turns into a karaoke bar in the evening, because that is Miranda's great passion. Eban Olliver has many jobs at once. He is the lighthouse keeper of Kingstown. And that means a lot of responsibility in St. Vincent's biggest port city. Because Eban doesn't just change the light bulbs, he's a pilot dispatcher, harbour master and guard all in one person. First contact with ships is particularly important. Once he even saved the entire nation from an Ebola virus.

programme.title: mareTV
programme.desc: Dramatically, the coast plunges into the sea, the so-called Costa of Peru is a gigantic desert on the Pacific Ocean, 2,400 kilometers long. Rugged, bizarre, like from another star. Ancient cultures settled here, the Moche and the Chimú. Later the proud Inca, the self-proclaimed royal people of the Andes, ruled. In the small town of Huanchaco in northern Peru, the fishermen build a new boat every month: from reed. The small ships are called Caballito de Totora (reed horses), because the shape is reminiscent of a horse and the fishermen ride in it kneeling in the waves. About 2,000 years ago, the moche used it to go out to sea. Carlos Segura Azola is a descendant of the moche. He is currently building a "reed horse" again. But actually something completely different occupies him. Carlos has been afraid of the sea ever since his father drowned at work. The fisherman consults a Brucha, a witch. She tries to drive him out of panic at the Pacific with mysterious ceremonies. At some point Carlos' reed boat is finished and he has to get out to his nets again. In the south, in the port city of Pisco, there are hardly any private cars. Mototaxis on three wheels are the means of transport here. Every moto looks different, because the young drivers compete with each other. They constantly pimp out their tricycles with spoilers, mirrors and all kinds of decorations. Because if you stand out in the traffic jam, you get the most passengers. But nowhere else in Peru is road traffic as dense and chaotic as in the capital Lima. That's why Juan Ortiz goes daily with his Delta glider over the coastal road into the air. He is the flying reporter and reports live for the traffic news. And he is not alone with his aircraft. Dozens of paragliders float along the cliffs of Lima at any time of the day. The Pacific provides a constant upwind on the up to 160 meter high cliffs. Somewhere in the labyrinth of the metropolis of millions hundreds of believers trot to the beat of the music of a Jesus statue on the cross. A procession for the "Lord of Miracles", the brass band intones church songs. The music student Bruno Alvarado drags and plays the largest instrument, a sousaphone. Every day there are dozens of processions in Lima. Practical for Bruno, he finances his studies with the musical accompaniment. Away from the hustle and bustle of the big city, in the Paracas National Park, it is deserted. Luis Zapato runs a kiosk on a lonely beach amidst huge sand dunes. The kiosk has meanwhile developed into a gourmet temple, even the capital city dwellers flock here. Especially because of the scallops that Luis is the only licence holder to collect far and wide from the seabed. Inti Mar he called his beach snack, Inti is the sun god, Mar the sea. At Luis you literally eat like a sun god at the sea. This is only possible here, on the Inca coast.

programme.title: The Heart of Hawaii
programme.desc: The Oahu island is the economic and cultural centre of the Hawaiian archipelago, as well as being a paradise for surfers, active holidaymakers and freedom lovers. The island is home to more than half of all natives and almost eight million foreign guests annually. Anyone searching for peace and seclusion won't find either in Honolulu or Waikiki. Traffic congestion and a backdrop of skyscrapers belong to the town panorama. Hip cafes, galleries and young fashion firms have settled here, adding a new and modern flair to the capital. Cindy's Lei Shoppe, the Chinatown florists, has been here for more than 30 years. This is also because the so-called Leis, the tradition of floral decoration, usually offered as a flower necklace or as a headdress, is still an important part of Hawaiian culture. Keone Nunes is well-known for his traditional tattooing skills, whereby the so-called Kakau Tattoos are virtually hammered into the skin with a hippo tooth. But Oahu is also an island of overwhelming natural beauty and a fascinating diversity on the smallest of places ...

programme.title: mareTV
programme.desc: Gigantic fjords and over 5,000 small islands: In Alaska's south there's a lot of wilderness and little civilization. The region between the capital Juneau and the Canadian border is also known by locals as the "Last Frontier", the last outpost. Michelle Masden, for example, has come to terms with the harsh conditions here. She is the only seaplane pilot in the region. She earns her living with her 65-year-old Beaver: as an air taxi, transport plane and now and then as a rescuer in last necessity. Michelle flies anything and everything: spare parts and food, doctors, hikers and hunters. For the fishermen of southern Alaska she searches for large shoals of fish and guides the captains to the next good catch. Ray Rusaw was a car mechanic by profession, but in his old age he was gripped by gold fever. With a self-built special raft and a highly creative selection of equipment from the DIY store, he sets off in search of the precious metal that his predecessors overlooked two centuries ago. For four years he has been happily obsessively combing through sandbanks and river courses. For Ray, the search for gold has little to do with luck and much to do with meticulous research. He is certain that he has found the perfect spot.

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: Iceland: Land of Fire and Ice
programme.desc: Africa is a land of epic natural spectacles and staggering beauty. It is also, truly, wild at heart. This series doesn't just present wildlife - it brings it to life, with the microscopic detail of the smallest ant, and the pounding heart of a lion.

programme.title: Wildest Middle East
programme.desc: Wildest Middle East reveals the remotest and most surprising corners of this ancient land, where you will find nothing is as it seems and where the natural world is fighting a constant battle to survive in an ever changing world.

programme.title: Wildest Places: Australia
programme.desc: Wildest Places explores some of the most incredible natural habitats on earth & a diverse range of wildlife.

programme.title: Wildest Indonesia
programme.desc: The islands of Indonesia are a wild paradise of giant flowers and coral gardens, of dragons, tigers, giant apes and glorious birds of paradise. A staggering fifteen percent of all the world's species are found in this unique region.

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Thailand – The Wild Heart
programme.desc: This 5 part series sweeps across the most diverse peninsular in the world. From Malaysia, Southwest China and Vietnam to Cambodia and Thailand this vast area includes outstanding landscapes, historic cities, tropical jungles and armies of animals.

programme.title: Wild Asia: Island Magic
programme.desc: A dramatic and poignant portrayal of the fragile alliances and desperate strategies for survival adopted by three species of animals caught in the no-man's-land where the human and natural worlds of Asia collide.

programme.title: Wildest Islands: Amazon River Islands: The Floating Forest
programme.desc: Islands can be home to the most extreme examples of life, as nature evolves in the most dramatic way. Sometimes isolated for hundreds of thousands of years, pockets of individuals adapt to survive, fuelling a rapid and continuous evolution of new species.

programme.title: Cuddly & Wild: Adorable Animal Babies
programme.desc: The first year in a wild animal's life is the most crucial! Will it get enough nourishment?

programme.title: Cute Little Killers
programme.desc: Meet maniacs, killers, and sweet-faced assassins in this fun intro to the world's rarest marsupials.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Light On Earth
programme.desc: Bioluminescence is everywhere—land, sea, and soil. Attenborough reveals its wonders!

programme.title: Dragons & Damsels
programme.desc: More beautiful than butterflies and better fliers than hummingbirds, dragonflies are ultimate insects.

programme.title: Eyes Of The Atacama
programme.desc: Atacama Desert, Chile: the driest, most inhospitable place to observe the birth of the universe.

programme.title: From Cubs To Kings
programme.desc: Thousands of miles from their home, a pair of tiny, new-born lion cubs are posing for tourist snapshots.

programme.title: Life in the Blue
programme.desc: This films reveals the extraordinary variety of life found in the vast blue expanses of the open ocean.

programme.title: Boteti
programme.desc: For the first time in 30 years, rain has fallen in sufficient quantities in the Angolan highlands.

programme.title: Croc Labyrinth
programme.desc: Two divers make unprecedented discoveries deep within the lair of the Nile crocodile.

programme.title: Electric Amazon
programme.desc: In the Amazon's rivers and streams, lurk fish armed with a highly sophisticated electric sixth sense.

programme.title: Wolf VS Bear
programme.desc: A mother gray wolf has a face-off with deadly brown bears to get food for her pups.

programme.title: Cheetahs of the Deep
programme.desc: This film documents the lives of short-finned pilot whales and reveals social behaviour unique to the species.

programme.title: Polar Bear Town Summer
programme.desc: Tourists flock to town to see thousands of beluga whales crowding the bay.

programme.title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
programme.desc: With more Caribou in Alaska than people, they're the lifeblood of many remote communities. But human expansion, and wild predators, have led to their rapid decline.

programme.title: Shamwari
programme.desc: Our wildlife team is in the line of fire once again when they become trapped in the middle of a rhino sandwich. Johan the vet has a rather tricky operation to perform. He needs to give one of our rescued lions a vasectomy.

programme.title: Shamwari
programme.desc: Johan crash tackles an adult cheetah after it fails to succumb to the tranquilizer and almost ends up falling asleep on the electric fence. Melvin, our baby giraffe is heading out into the bush to sample leaves for the first time.

programme.title: Shamwari
programme.desc: We need to capture five lions to move to another game reserve. Out on the reserve we manage to capture some incredible footage of a baby rhino feeding. And walking with hippo, how to stay alive when you are facing Africa's biggest killer on foot.

programme.title: Animal Society
programme.desc: Ancient as the dinosaurs themselves take a look at the family structure of crocodilians, turtles and snakes.

programme.title: Animal Society
programme.desc: The great cats are a group of apex predators that rest firmly at the top of the food chain. Lions, leopards and tigers all live uniquely with particular hunting prowess, advantages and pack hierarchy or solitary territories.

programme.title: Blue Realm
programme.desc: Believe it or not, many scuba divers want to see great whites up close, and photographers want dramatic shots. But how do you get the sharks to where you want them?

programme.title: Animal Ambulance

programme.title: EXO 'REVERXE' Trailer
programme.desc: At the end of the trailer, the title of EXO's 8th full-length album, “REVERXE,” was finally revealed. Based on EXO's universe storyline — “the day we truly become one, a new world will open” — the album name reinterprets the word “Reverse” in EXO's own style, carrying the meaning “a new world opens once again,” further heightening anticipation.EXO has returned with a new trailer.

programme.title: JUNGWOO 'SUGAR' MV
programme.desc: NCT JUNGWOO's first solo single “SUGAR” is a groovy pop song with swing‑infused drums, a witty bassline, and a smooth, catchy melody. The lyrics send a warm message of gratitude to fans, wishing that past memories and future moments are all sweet. The music video brings this sweetness to life with colorful, sugar-inspired visuals, while the performance highlights JUNGWOO's stylish movements and precise choreography, showcasing his unique stage presence.

programme.title: RIIZE 'Fame' MV
programme.desc: The lyrics reflect RIIZE's artistic vision, delivering the message that what they truly seek is not fame, but the sharing of emotions and love.The bold, forward-driving rhythmic structure combined with the raw texture of electric guitar creates a dynamic surge of energy. RIIZE's second single “Fame” is the group's first venture into a rage-style hip-hop track, expressing their artistic ideals as “emotional pop artists.”

programme.title: WayV '白色定格 (Eternal White)' MV
programme.desc: The title track “白色定格 (Eternal White)” is a dance-pop song that blends a rhythmic saxophone riff with energetic drum beats, creating a warm atmosphere through the harmony of powerful rap and sweet vocals. The lyrics express a strong determination to break free from time frozen like a white winter and to keep moving forward, capturing the hearts of listeners..

programme.title: TAEYEON 'Panorama' MV
programme.desc: TAEYEON celebrates the 10th anniversary of her solo debut with the compilation album Panorama, featuring the Pop Rock title track “인사 (Panorama).” The song pairs vibrant piano, synths, and a strong drum line with soaring guitar riffs and TAEYEON's delicate yet powerful vocals. The music video presents a cinematic story of TAEYEON, who has lost her memory, and a girl who helps restore it — a metaphor for the bond between the artist and her fans, SONEs. Its emotional, road-movie-style visuals reveal SONEs as the hidden protagonist. The 24-track album offers a retrospective of her past decade, unfolding like a panorama of her solo journey.

programme.title: TAEYEON The 10th Anniversary Special
programme.desc: Experience a panoramic journey through the past decade of TAEYEON's career, a vocalist who seamlessly transitions between delicate sensitivity and powerful intensity.
